deep_dive.title: Dynamic Rowhammer Threshold Management:Temperature-Aware Threshold Degradation for In-DRAM Defenses
deep_dive.abstract: In-DRAM Rowhammer defenses pin the mitigation threshold at manufacture time, yet the true Rowhammer Threshold (TRHD) varies with runtime temperature. We propose Dynamic Rowhammer Threshold Management, a defense-agnostic runtime layer that re-sources each defense's threshold from the observed temperature once per epoch via a linear-$T$ model with a VRD-motivated guardband $g$, projecting the result onto SALT-C, PRAC, and TRR through each defense's threshold parameter. A decoupled oracle that scales physical TRHD per-DIMM by $δ\sim \mathrm{N}(1, σ)$ breaks model self-consistency. The layer drives PRAC's 72 staleness breaches at 85$^\circ$C to zero; at $σ{=}0.10$, sweeping $g$ collapses PRAC breaches from 38.4 ($g{=}1.0$) to 9.6 ($g{=}0.9$). SALT-C drops from 10 nominal-static breaches to 2 (Dynamic) to 0 (bootstrap), at $\leq$5.1\% latency. TRR is capacity-limited; the layer acts as a diagnostic.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Dynamic Rowhammer threshold management defends against kernel privilege escalation attacks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 动态Rowhammer阈值管理防御内核提权攻击。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 动态Rowhammer阈值管理防御内核提权攻击。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 动态Rowhammer阈值管理防御内核提权攻击。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: The paper proposes a runtime layer that dynamically adjusts Rowhammer mitigation thresholds based on temperature, eliminating staleness breaches in PRAC and improving defense reliability, thus strengthening kernel protection against Rowhammer attacks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有Rowhammer防御在制造时固定阈值，但真实阈值随温度变化，导致高温下防御失效。本文提出动态阈值管理层，根据运行时温度线性调整阈值，消除PRAC在85°C下的72次过期违规，并将噪声下的违规次数从38.4降至9。该方法可普遍增强行锤防御，提升系统安全性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有Rowhammer防御在制造时固定阈值，但真实阈值随温度变化，导致高温下防御失效。本文提出动态阈值管理层，根据运行时温度线性调整阈值，消除PRAC在85°C下的72次过期违规，并将噪声下的违规次数从38.4降至9。该方法可普遍增强行锤防御，提升系统安全性。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: How to defend against kernel level attacks in modern operating systems

deep_dive.title: A Measurement Study of AI-Environment Realism Gaps in Malware-Analysis Sandboxes
deep_dive.abstract: Sandboxing remains a core technique for observing suspicious program behavior, yet environment-aware malware increasingly suppresses execution when analysis is suspected. Prior generations of sandbox evasion focused on virtualization artifacts, timing discrepancies, and wear-and-tear realism. In this paper, we present the first systematic measurement study of AI-environment artifacts as a new sandbox-evasion surface. We operationalize this realism gap through AIprint, a probe framework that captures persistent artifacts left behind by AI-capable software ecosystems, including AI-assistant configuration directories, model caches, environment variables, local inference services, and package dependencies.   We systematically extract 450 unique artifacts from 284 open-source AI projects on GitHub, compile them into unprivileged Windows probes, and evaluate them across seven commercial and open-source sandbox backends together with three AI-capable reference hosts. Our results show that traditional VM-detection baselines fail to reliably distinguish real AI-capable systems from modern sandboxes, whereas twelve AI-environment artifacts appear on the reference hosts and on none of the evaluated backends. A controlled 214-step installation experiment establishes a causal relationship between AI tool and package installation and measurable AI-environment artifact accumulation, while adaptive spoofing experiments reveal a fundamental operational asymmetry: reproducing convincing AI software environments is substantially more expensive than detecting shallow spoofing.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: systematic measurement of AI-environment artifacts as sandbox evasion surface
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 系统化测量AI环境遗留作为沙箱逃避的新特征面
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 系统化测量AI环境遗留作为沙箱逃避的新特征面
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 系统化测量AI环境遗留作为沙箱逃避的新特征面
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces AIprint, a probe framework to measure AI-environment artifacts that malware can abuse to evade sandbox analysis.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对环境感知恶意软件利用AI环境新特征逃避沙箱检测的问题，提出AIPrint探针框架，系统化提取450个独特AI环境遗留伪影，并测量沙箱逃避现实性差距。研究揭示了AI软件生态在沙箱中留下的配置、缓存等新逃避面，为提升动态分析沙箱的真实性与鲁棒性提供了重要依据。此外，研究还通过真实恶意软件样本验证了AI伪影逃避的有效性，为沙箱设计者提供了具体改进方向。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对环境感知恶意软件利用AI环境新特征逃避沙箱检测的问题，提出AIPrint探针框架，系统化提取450个独特AI环境遗留伪影，并测量沙箱逃避现实性差距。研究揭示了AI软件生态在沙箱中留下的配置、缓存等新逃避面，为提升动态分析沙箱的真实性与鲁棒性提供了重要依据。此外，研究还通过真实恶意软件样本验证了AI伪影逃避的有效性，为沙箱设计者提供了具体改进方向。

deep_dive.title: Automated Template-free Synthesis of Instruction-Centric Leakage Contracts for Black-Box CPUs
deep_dive.abstract: Side-channel attacks pose a significant security threat for modern computing platforms, because they exploit subtle discrepancies in CPU behaviors to leak sensitive information. To model the information leaked by a CPU via microarchitectural side-channels, recent work proposed leakage contracts: an ISA-level security abstraction that provides the foundations for secure CPU programming. Unfortunately, due to the complexity of current microarchitectures, devising a leakage contract for a CPU requires extensive manual effort and thus modern CPUs lack dedicated leakage contracts.   We present a methodology to extract instruction-centric leakage contracts for major CPU architectures with minimal manual intervention. We implemented this technique in malcos, the first template-free tool that automates the synthesis of leakage contracts for black-box CPUs. We evaluate malcos on x86 and ARM CPUs, and show that the contracts it synthesizes are precise and sound with respect to all leaks observed during synthesis. Our results demonstrate that learning leakage contracts from black-box CPUs is feasible.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Automatically synthesizes instruction-centric leakage contracts by observing CPU execution, enabling dynamic analysis of microarchitectural side-channels
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过观察CPU执行自动合成指令中心泄露合约，支持对微架构侧信道的动态分析
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过观察CPU执行自动合成指令中心泄露合约，支持对微架构侧信道的动态分析
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过观察CPU执行自动合成指令中心泄露合约，支持对微架构侧信道的动态分析
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper addresses the lack of automated leakage contracts for CPUs by introducing a template-free methodology to synthesize instruction-centric leakage contracts for black-box CPUs, providing a foundation for secure programming against microarchitectural side-channel attacks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现代CPU缺乏专用的泄漏合约来应对微架构侧信道攻击，且现有手工设计方法复杂。本文提出一种自动化方法，无需模板即可为黑盒CPU架构提取指令为中心的泄漏合约，通过观察执行行为建模信息泄漏。该方法为安全编程提供了 ISA 级的安全抽象，有助于防御侧信道攻击，对运行时动态分析技术具有重要价值。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现代CPU缺乏专用的泄漏合约来应对微架构侧信道攻击，且现有手工设计方法复杂。本文提出一种自动化方法，无需模板即可为黑盒CPU架构提取指令为中心的泄漏合约，通过观察执行行为建模信息泄漏。该方法为安全编程提供了 ISA 级的安全抽象，有助于防御侧信道攻击，对运行时动态分析技术具有重要价值。
